steps.title: Find the key signal in the passage
steps.content: The passage says Klein created images with elements “generally seen as flaws” — deliberate rule-breaking. It also calls his work “so unorthodox” that publishers rejected it. We need a word meaning he deliberately broke from conventions.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — reviewed: “Reviewed” means analyzed or evaluated. Klein didn’t examine photography’s conventions — he broke them. ✗
  • Choice B — defied: “Defied” means deliberately resisted or disobeyed. This fits Klein’s intentional use of elements considered flaws. ✓
  • Choice C — respected: “Respected” means admired or followed. Klein clearly did the opposite, using features deemed flaws. ✗
  • Choice D — prevented: “Prevented” means stopped. Klein didn’t stop conventions from existing — he simply ignored them. ✗
That is the correct answer.
